generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSpeedController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Controls motor voltage via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for adaptive cruise control (ACC) system"
]

instance SteeringServoController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Sends steering servo commands through lower-level chassis control system for vehicle positioning"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor (Hokuyo UST-10LX) for detecting objects in vehicle's environment with 360° coverage"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARObjectDetectionData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera (Intel Deepsense D435i) providing additional environmental perception with stereo vision capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraObjectDetectionData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Combination of speed and steering angle sensors for real-time vehicle state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARObjectDetectionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR
]

instance CameraObjectDetectionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance PointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Point cloud clustering and tracking algorithms (DBSCAN + Kalman filter) for object detection and trajectory prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ACCControlLogic : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 15622 compliant ACC control logic for performance class I adaptive cruise control system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetpack50SDK
]

instance ObjectTrackDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "SQL database storing historical object tracks and vehicle state data externally to the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARObjectDetectionData, CameraObjectDetectionData, VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudBasedStorage
]

instance RealTimeObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current object tracks (position, velocity, size) from sensor fusion for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARObjectDetectionData, CameraObjectDetectionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VehicleStateHistory : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical vehicle state data (speed, steering angle) for pattern analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudBasedStorage
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPath : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calculated projected driving path based on current vehicle state and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 for middleware and communication between vehicle components"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService, RemoteManagementService, DataCollectionService
]

instance NVIDIAJetpack50SDK : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for running machine learning models on Jetson Orin Nano platform"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingModel, ACCControlLogic
]

instance PythonPrototyping : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 environment for rapid prototyping of digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for complex systems modeling including point cloud clustering algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingModel
]

instance CloudBasedStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud infrastructure for storing and processing historical data offloading computation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackDatabase, VehicleStateHistory
]

instance WiFiNetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi network enabling inter-entity communication between vehicle and cloud components"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of vehicle state, object tracks, and projected driving path"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote operator interface for enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speeds, and triggering emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides DriverControlAction, RemoteACCManagementAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud-based execution of ACC system logic with results sent back to vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCOptimizationRecommendation
]

instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of human-readable reports of vehicle state and object tracks for analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisReport
]

instance EmergencyResponseService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatic triggering of emergency braking commands based on detected safety conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakingCommand
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of vehicle performance and object detection visualized in 3D environment"
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Developer-generated data reports for system troubleshooting and improvement"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical analysis of driving patterns and object interactions for performance optimization"
]

instance ACCOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimization recommendations for adaptive cruise control (ACC) parameters based on real-time data"
]

instance EmergencyBrakingEventAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of emergency braking events to improve system response and safety protocols"
]

instance DriverControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Driver decisions based on real-time visualizations of vehicle and environment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RemoteACCManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote operators enabling/disabling ACC or setting desired speeds via control panel"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic triggering of emergency braking commands based on detected safety threats"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C17: Environment
instance VehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor, VehicleSpeedController, SteeringServoController
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetpack50SDK, PythonPrototyping, MATLABSimulink, CloudBasedStorage
]

instance UserInterface : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeVisualizationService, RemoteManagementService, DataCollectionService
]

instance CrossSystemInterface : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains WiFiNetwork
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT interacts with the physical twin through:
  - Remote management commands (enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, emergency braking)
  - Computation offloading: ACC control logic is executed in the DT cloud, with results sent back to the vehicle's ECU
  - Actuation commands: Desired speed commands are converted to motor voltage and steering servo commands sent to the vehicle hardware."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates with a real-time requirement of:
  - End-to-end latency ≤ 100 ms between vehicle and DT Entity
  - Clock synchronization accuracy of ≤ 100 ms across distributed components
  The system is designed for low-latency interaction critical for autonomous driving applications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
  - Architecture follows ISO 23247 reference architecture with custom modifications:
    - Physical Twin Entity (custom) handles sensor data processing and actuation
    - Digital Twin Entity implements core DT logic (data collection, reporting, ACC control)
    - User Entity split into visualization and control components
    - Cross-System Entity handles data translation between entities
  - Communication: ROS2 network for intra-entity communication, Wi-Fi for inter-entity communication
  - Deployment: Physical components on vehicle hardware, DT components on cloud/server infrastructure."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  - Designed Phase: Digital twin architecture and requirements defined during system specification
  - Manufactured Phase: Physical vehicle built with autonomy components and sensors
  - Operated Phase: DT used for real-time monitoring, remote management, and optimization during vehicle operation
  The DT covers the 'as operated' lifecycle phase with real-time capabilities."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  - Verification:
    - Latency requirements verified through testing (≤ 100 ms)
    - Clock synchronization accuracy verified
    - ACC control logic verified against ISO 15622 standards
  - Validation:
    - Real-world testing with 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle
    - Visualization accuracy validated through on-board camera comparison
    - Object tracking accuracy validated through point cloud processing
  - Uncertainty:
    - Model uncertainty in sensor fusion algorithms
    - Environmental uncertainty in LiDAR and camera perception
    - Computational uncertainty in cloud-based processing"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  - Network Protocols:
    - ROS2 network for intra-entity communication (ROS 2.0)
    - Wi-Fi network for inter-entity communication (802.11 standard)
  - Communication Architecture:
    - ROS2 nodes for component communication within each entity
    - Data Translation FE handles message serialization and unit conversion
    - End-to-end latency ≤ 100 ms between vehicle and DT Entity"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  - Deployment Architecture:
    - Physical Twin components deployed on vehicle hardware (NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, ROS2, MATLAB/Simulink)
    - Digital Twin components deployed on cloud/server infrastructure
    - User interface components deployed on remote client devices
  - Hosting Infrastructure:
    - Cloud-based DT hosting for computation offloading
    - External storage for historical data (SQL database)
    - Public demonstration available through online platform"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT integrates with:
  - External vehicle data systems (via ROS2 middleware)
  - Cloud-based analytics platforms for data storage and processing
  - Potential future integration with other automotive digital twins for fleet coordination
  - Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication standards for future expansion"
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
  - Data Ownership:
    - Vehicle owner owns raw sensor data collected from the physical twin
    - DT service provider owns processed data and derived insights
  - Privacy Considerations:
    - Data is stored externally to the vehicle for monitoring purposes
    - Visualization data is presented in a way that minimizes privacy risks
    - No explicit mention of data anonymization or encryption protocols in documents"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  - Standards Followed:
    - ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework) as reference architecture
    - ISO 15622 for ACC control system requirements
    - 3GPP Technical Specification 22.185 for latency requirements
    - ROS2 standards for middleware communication
    - IEC 62541 (OPC UA) for potential future data translation implementation
  - Custom Modifications:
    - Custom Physical Twin Entity (not defined in ISO 23247)
    - Split User Interface FE into visualization and control components"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
  - Safety:
    - System designed for safety-critical vehicular applications
    - Latency requirements (≤ 100 ms) ensure safe operation
    - ACC control follows ISO 15622 standards for performance class I
    - Potential risk of remote operation on autonomous vehicle requires careful validation
  - Security:
    - Wi-Fi network used for inter-entity communication (potential security vulnerability)
    - No explicit mention of encryption protocols for data transmission
    - Cloud-based computation offloading requires secure access controls
    - Data storage on external devices requires protection against unauthorized access"
]
